description: Claude Code usage — cost, tokens, cache efficiency, and development output
title: Claude Code

options.content:
### 💵 About the dollar figures on this dashboard
All **cost / $ values here are API-equivalent estimates** — what this usage **would** cost at pay-as-you-go [API list prices](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/pricing) **if you had no subscription**. **They are NOT a bill.** On a Pro / Max / Team plan you pay a **flat monthly fee** and are not charged these amounts — a high number means you're getting strong value from your plan. For your real remaining quota, run **`/usage`** in the Claude Code CLI.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Burn Rate
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. This is the pay-as-you-go API list price this usage would cost if you had no subscription. On a Pro/Max/Team plan you pay a flat monthly fee and are NOT charged this. Current rate is projected from the past 30 minutes. Green below $0.50/hr, yellow to $2/hr, red above.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cost
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. What the last 24 hours of usage would cost at pay-as-you-go API list prices if you had no subscription. On a Pro/Max/Team plan you pay a flat monthly fee, not this amount. Shown alongside the 7-day rolling daily average to see if today is above or below baseline.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Subagent Cost
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. The pay-as-you-go API list price of subagent tasks — parallel research, code review, and background work — over the selected time range. On a subscription plan you are not charged this. High subagent cost relative to main session cost means Claude is spawning many parallel workers.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Main Session Cost
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. The pay-as-you-go API list price of main conversation turns (your prompts and Claude's responses, excluding subagent and auxiliary tasks) over the selected time range. On a subscription plan you pay a flat fee, not this amount.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cost Forecast
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Daily and monthly projections of what your usage WOULD cost at pay-as-you-go API list prices, extrapolated from the current 6-hour burn rate. On a Pro/Max/Team plan your real bill is a flat monthly fee regardless of these numbers — a high projection here means you are extracting strong value from your plan, not that you owe this much.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cost / Session
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Average pay-as-you-go API list price per session over the selected time range, vs the 7-day average. On a subscription plan you are not charged per session. A rising number usually means sessions are running longer without being compacted.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cost per 1K Tokens
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Effective pay-as-you-go API list price per 1,000 tokens across all token types. On a subscription plan you do not pay per token. Because cache reads are ~10% of input price, heavy cache usage pulls this number down. Compare against the 7-day average to spot efficiency changes.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cost by Effort Level
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Pay-as-you-go API list price grouped by the effort setting (low / medium / high / max) over the selected time range; on a subscription plan you are not charged this. Effort controls thinking-token budget. A large share at high or max effort is worth checking against whether those tasks actually needed deep reasoning.

panels.title: API-Equivalent Cache Savings
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT FIGURE, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Approximate dollars prompt caching would save at pay-as-you-go API list prices over the selected time range, vs the 7-day average. On a subscription plan this is value/efficiency, not cash back. Computed as cacheRead tokens × (input price − cache read price): Sonnet $2.70/1M, Haiku $0.72/1M. Sonnet dominates usage here.

panels.title: Peak Cost Hours (API-Equivalent)
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Pay-as-you-go API list price per hour over the selected range. On a subscription plan you are not charged this. Spikes show when sessions were most expensive — useful for correlating high-usage periods with what was being worked on.

panels.title: Usage Anomaly Detection
panels.description: Deviation of current hourly usage (measured via API-equivalent cost) from the 7-day historical average for the same hour. This is a relative usage signal, not a dollar amount you are billed. Values above 200% (dashed line) are flagged as anomalies — investigate what was running during those periods.

panels.title: Effective Tokens — All Models
panels.description: Actual effective (non-cacheRead) tokens consumed across all models over the selected time range, with a 7-day sparkline showing the daily trend. These are the tokens that count toward Anthropic's usage limits (cacheRead is excluded). This is a real measured volume, not a percentage of limit — OTel telemetry cannot reconstruct Anthropic's quota gauge because the weekly reset boundary is not exported. For your true remaining quota, run /usage in the Claude Code CLI.

panels.title: Effective Tokens — Sonnet Only
panels.description: Actual effective (non-cacheRead) Sonnet tokens consumed over the selected time range, with a 7-day sparkline trend. Sonnet has its own separate usage cap at Anthropic, which is why it's broken out here. This is a real measured volume, not a percentage of limit — the weekly reset boundary needed to compute a true percentage is not exported in OTel telemetry. Run /usage in the Claude Code CLI for your actual remaining quota.

panels.title: Total Tokens
panels.description: All tokens consumed over the selected time range across all types, alongside the 7-day daily average.

panels.title: Weekly Total Token Usage
panels.description: Total tokens consumed in the last 7 days. The sparkline shows the daily trend — a rising slope means usage is accelerating week over week.

panels.title: Token Usage Rate (24h)
panels.description: Tokens consumed per minute across all types. The legend table shows mean, last, and peak rates for the selected window. A sustained high rate approaching your plan's TPM ceiling is the signal to start a fresh session.

panels.title: Effective Tokens by Source
panels.description: Hourly effective (non-cacheRead) tokens stacked by query_source: main (direct conversation turns), auxiliary (background context builds), and subagent (parallel spawned agents). A rising subagent share means Claude is doing more autonomous orchestration relative to interactive work.

panels.title: Token Distribution by Model
panels.description: Share of total tokens consumed by each model over the selected time range. Sonnet dominating is expected for most workloads. A large Opus slice warrants a check on whether those tasks needed Opus-level reasoning.

panels.title: Cache Hit Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of input-side tokens served from Anthropic's prompt cache. Above 80% is healthy. Below 60% means cache is not being reused effectively — sessions may be too short or context structure is breaking cache blocks.

panels.title: Code Edit Acceptance Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of Claude's proposed file edits that were accepted over the selected time range, plus the 7-day average. Below 80% suggests edits are frequently being rejected or need rework.

panels.title: Active Time
panels.description: CLI time is how long Claude Code was running and processing. User time is how long you were actively engaged. A high CLI-to-user ratio means Claude is doing a lot of autonomous work.

panels.title: Active Sessions
panels.description: Number of Claude Code sessions over the selected time range compared to the 7-day daily average.

panels.title: Lines of Code Modified
panels.description: Lines added and removed over the selected time range alongside rolling 7-day daily averages. A large gap between Today and 7 Day Avg indicates an unusually active or quiet day.

panels.title: Average Session Metrics
panels.description: Per-session averages over the selected time range: cost in dollars, total tokens, and active CLI time. Rising cost or token averages over days indicate sessions are running longer without being compacted.

panels.title: Code Modification Velocity (lines/min)
panels.description: Rate of lines added and removed per minute. Spikes indicate concentrated editing bursts. A high removed rate relative to added suggests refactoring or cleanup sessions.

panels.title: Commits
panels.description: Git commits made via Claude Code over the selected time range versus the 7-day daily average. Pairs with the lines-of-code panels to show shipped output, not just edit volume.

panels.title: Model Token Efficiency (tokens/$)
panels.description: Total tokens per dollar spent by model over the selected time range. Higher is more efficient. Haiku should significantly outperform Opus. If the gap is narrow, check whether model selection is being forced.

panels.title: Tool Usage Breakdown
panels.description: Share of tool calls by tool type over the selected time range, sourced from structured logs. Covers all tools — Bash, Read, Edit, Write, and others — not just file edits.

panels.title: Tool Decision Sources
panels.description: How tool execution decisions were authorized over the selected time range. Config = approved via CLAUDE.md or settings. User temporary = approved once for the session. User permanent = added to always-allow list.

panels.title: MCP Server Token Attribution
panels.description: Token consumption attributed to MCP server calls over the selected time range. Only turns that invoked an MCP tool carry the mcp_server_name label. Shows which MCP integrations (e.g. linear) are driving context size and cost.

panels.title: Top Skills by API-Equivalent Cost
panels.description: API-EQUIVALENT COST, NOT YOUR ACTUAL BILL. Pay-as-you-go API list price attributed to named skills over the selected time range; on a subscription plan you are not charged this. The skill_name label is set whenever Claude Code invokes a skill. Turns without a skill are excluded. High-cost skills often have large context windows or expensive prompt templates worth reviewing.

panels.title: Top Skills by Effective Tokens
panels.description: Effective (non-cacheRead) token consumption per skill over the selected time range. A skill with high tokens here but low cost in the panel to the left is getting strong cache reuse. A skill high in both is a genuine cost driver.

panels.title: Prompts Per Hour
panels.description: Count of user prompt events logged over a rolling 1-hour window, sourced from structured logs. Peaks show concentrated interaction periods; gaps are idle time between sessions.

panels.title: Prompt Length Distribution (chars)
panels.description: Distribution of prompt lengths by character count bucket over the selected time range, sourced from structured logs. Most prompts are short — long prompts (1k+ chars) typically mean pasted code or detailed task descriptions.

panels.title: Response Latency p95 by Model
panels.description: 95th percentile response time per request by model over the selected range, from structured request logs. This is how long Anthropic's servers take to respond — a real performance signal for everyone, independent of plan or billing. Higher Sonnet latency vs Haiku reflects longer reasoning chains; sustained spikes above baseline signal context-window pressure or server backpressure. Yellow at 30s, red at 60s.

panels.title: Request Errors
panels.description: Count of failed requests over the selected time range — including rate-limit rejections, network errors, and model errors returned by Anthropic's servers. Applies to everyone regardless of plan or billing. Green at zero; any errors turn the panel yellow, 5+ turns it red. Drill into the Logs dashboard for detail.
